comments.author: User
comments.text: can you check for me if amazon sphere is open on august 1st Saturday? and do I need to make a reservation?

comments.author: Agent Reasoning
comments.text:
```text
Reasoning completed in 4 steps
Determining date relevance
I am confirming that August 1, 2026, falls on a Saturday and consider searching for information on the Amazon Spheres in Seattle.
Searches
OK, I'll search for 'Amazon Spheres Seattle public visits reservations hours August 1 2026 Saturday'...
Searches
OK, I'll search for 'Amazon Spheres Seattle visit reservations official'...
Planning reservation details
I'm calculating that August 1, 2026, is the first Saturday and noting that reservations might open on July 17, 2026, at 10 AM Pacific time.
```

comments.author: Agent Answer
comments.text:
Yes — **Amazon’s Seattle Spheres should be open to the public on Saturday, August 1, 2026**, because public visits are offered on the **first and third Saturday of each month**, from **10:00 AM–6:00 PM Pacific Time**; August 1, 2026 is the **first Saturday**. [\[seattlespheres.com\]](https:www.seattlespheres.com/the-spheres-weekend-public-visits)
And yes, **you need a reservation**. Public visits are **free**, but entry requires a confirmed reservation; reservations become available **15 days before the visit date**. [\[seattlespheres.com\]](https:www.seattlespheres.com/the-spheres-weekend-public-visits), [\[aboutamazon.com\]](https:www.aboutamazon.com/news/workplace/how-to-visit-the-amazon-spheres-in-seattle)
For **August 1, 2026**, reservations should open around **Friday, July 17, 2026**. Amazon says reservations are posted in real time 15 days in advance, and spots can fill quickly. [\[aboutamazon.com\]](https:www.aboutamazon.com/news/workplace/how-to-visit-the-amazon-spheres-in-seattle)
**Booking page:** Go to the official Spheres public visits page and use **“Book a Visit”**:  
<https:www.seattlespheres.com/the-spheres-weekend-public-visits> [\[seattlespheres.com\]](https:www.seattlespheres.com/the-spheres-weekend-public-visits)
A few quick notes:
* Address: **2111 7th Avenue, Seattle, WA 98121**. [\[seattlespheres.com\]](https:www.seattlespheres.com/the-spheres-weekend-public-visits)
* Adults **18+ need a valid government ID** at check-in. [\[seattlespheres.com\]](https:www.seattlespheres.com/the-spheres-weekend-public-visits)
* Visits are typically limited to about **one hour**. [\[seattlespheres.com\]](https:www.seattlespheres.com/the-spheres-weekend-public-visits)
* Large bags over **12” x 12”** are not allowed. [\[seattlespheres.com\]](https:www.seattlespheres.com/the-spheres-weekend-public-visits)

comments.author: User
comments.text: does a airline carry on bag qualify for "Large bags over 12” x 12” are not allowed"?

comments.author: Agent Reasoning
comments.text:
```text
Reasoning completed in 1 step
Clarifying carry-on rules
I am determining carry-on bag dimensions, noting that standard sizes typically exceed the limit of 12"x12." I will suggest alternatives like leaving bags at a hotel or locker.
```

comments.author: Agent Answer
comments.text:
Yes — a typical **airline carry-on bag would almost certainly be considered too large** for Amazon Spheres’ bag policy.
The Spheres’ visitor guidelines say to **limit your bag size to 12” × 12” or less**.  Most airline carry-ons are larger than that — for example, a common carry-on size is around **22” × 14” × 9”**, so it would exceed the Spheres’ allowed bag size. [\[seattlespheres.com\]](https:www.seattlespheres.com/the-spheres-weekend-public-visits)
**Best plan:** don’t bring a carry-on suitcase to your reservation. Bring only a small purse/crossbody/day bag that fits within **12” × 12”**, and store your luggage at your hotel, car, or a luggage-storage service before visiting. Adults in your group should also bring a valid government ID for check-in. [\[seattlespheres.com\]](https:www.seattlespheres.com/the-spheres-weekend-public-visits)

comments.author: User
comments.text: I mean a backpack that fits under the front seat

comments.author: Agent Answer
comments.text:
A **backpack that fits under an airplane seat may still be too large** for Amazon Spheres, depending on its dimensions.
Their rule is **bag size must be 12” × 12” or less**.  Many “personal item” backpacks that fit under a plane seat are often closer to **16–18 inches tall**, so they may exceed the Spheres limit even if they’re airline-approved. [\[seattlespheres.com\]](https:www.seattlespheres.com/the-spheres-weekend-public-visits)
**How to decide:**
* If your backpack is **12” × 12” or smaller** when packed: likely okay.
* If it’s taller than **12 inches** in any main dimension: I’d assume it may **not** be allowed.
* A small purse, sling bag, mini backpack, or compact daypack is safer.
If you’re traveling with luggage, I’d plan to leave the backpack/carry-on at your hotel, car, or luggage storage before the visit—just to avoid being turned away at check-in.

comments.text: can you search and summarized if interviewee get complimentary access to the amazon sphere?

comments.author: Agent Reasoning
comments.text:
```text

Gathering citation requirements

```

comments.author: Agent Answer
comments.text:
I searched for official Amazon/Spheres info and didn’t find any official statement saying **Amazon interviewees/candidates automatically get complimentary access to the Amazon Spheres**. The official Spheres page only describes two clear access paths: **public visits by reservation on the first/third Saturday** and **badged Amazon employees on weekdays**, with employee guest registration handled internally. [\[seattlespheres.com\]](https:www.seattlespheres.com/the-spheres-weekend-public-visits)
The public visit is **free**, but it still requires a confirmed reservation; reservations open **15 days before** the visit date. [\[seattlespheres.com\]](https:www.seattlespheres.com/the-spheres-weekend-public-visits), [\[aboutamazon.com\]](https:www.aboutamazon.com/news/workplace/how-to-visit-the-amazon-spheres-in-seattle)
Amazon’s interview/candidate pages explain the interview process and say your **recruiter/scheduler** handles interview logistics, but I did not see anything there about Spheres access being included for candidates. [\[amazon.jobs\]](https:www.amazon.jobs/content/en/how-we-hire/), [\[aboutamazon.com\]](https:www.aboutamazon.com/news/workplace/amazon-interview-process-phone-screens-loops)
**Bottom line:** Don’t assume interviewee status gives you Spheres access. If your interview is onsite in Seattle, the safest move is to ask your Amazon recruiter or scheduler something like:
> “Since I’ll be onsite for my interview, is there any opportunity to visit the Spheres, or should I book a public reservation separately?”
If they don’t explicitly arrange it, plan to book the normal free public reservation.
